numpy.acos.notes: NumSharp exposes the canonical NumPy name arccos.

numpy.asin.notes: NumSharp exposes the canonical NumPy name arcsin.

numpy.atan.notes: NumSharp exposes the canonical NumPy name arctan.

numpy.atan2.notes: NumSharp exposes the canonical NumPy name arctan2.

numpy.astype.notes: The NumPy 2.x top-level helper is available as the NDArray instance method.

numpy.bitwise_invert.notes: NumSharp exposes the ufunc alias invert.

numpy.bitwise_left_shift.notes: NumSharp exposes the ufunc alias left_shift.

numpy.bitwise_right_shift.notes: NumSharp exposes the ufunc alias right_shift.

numpy.cumulative_prod.notes: NumSharp exposes the established cumprod spelling.

numpy.cumulative_sum.notes: NumSharp exposes the established cumsum spelling.

numpy.pow.notes: NumSharp exposes the ufunc name power.

numpy.remainder.notes: NumSharp exposes the equivalent ufunc name mod.

numpy.round.notes: C# uses round_ to avoid a naming collision while preserving NumPy round semantics.

numpy.row_stack.notes: NumPy documents row_stack as an alias of vstack.

numpy.ndarray.itemsize.notes: The element size is exposed as dtypesize in NumSharp.

numpy.ndarray.data.notes: NumSharp exposes typed unmanaged data through the generic Data<T>() method rather than NumPy's buffer-view property.

numpy.ndarray.round.notes: Use the static np.round_ API; NumSharp does not expose ndarray.round as an instance member.

numpy.random.ranf.notes: NumPy documents ranf as an alias of random_sample.

numpy.random.sample.notes: NumPy documents sample as an alias of random_sample.

numpy.random.random_integers.notes: Use randint and adjust the upper bound; deprecated random_integers includes the high endpoint.

numpy.shape.notes: NumSharp exposes shape as an NDArray Shape property rather than the top-level NumPy tuple-returning helper.

numpy.linalg.matrix_power.notes: Matrix power is available as an NDArray instance method rather than under an linalg namespace.

numpy.ndarray.data.notes: Data<T>() returns a typed NumSharp ArraySlice and is not NumPy's Python buffer object.

numpy.random.random_integers.notes: The available randint alternative has an exclusive upper bound, while random_integers historically used an inclusive upper bound.

numpy.shape.notes: NDArray.Shape is a NumSharp Shape value, not the tuple returned by numpy.shape.

numpy.complex64.notes: The public compatibility symbol exists, but NumSharp does not implement a Complex64 storage dtype.
